**Gemini Enterprise Demo Script** 



**Scenario/ Storyline:**   

A Loan Officer leverages Gemini Enterprise to automate intricate loan processing workflows. The demo highlights the seamless orchestration between a low-code Agent Designer (AD) and a high-code Agent Development Kit (ADK), showcasing advanced content generation and end-to-end workflow automation.



| Gemini Enterprise Demo  ([Jamie de Guerre](mailto:jamiedeguerre@google.com)) In our previous engagement, we previewed the strategic trajectory of Gemini Enterprise: transitioning from an assistant interface to a comprehensive Agentic Orchestration Platform. This allows organizations to move beyond simple chat into autonomous workflows and custom-built agents, all governed within a single, unified ecosystem. To ground this in a real-world enterprise scenario, we’ll examine Meridian National Bank—the institution that Mike was working with which processes mortgage applications  (refer to Agent Platform demo.  Mortgage underwriting is a data-intensive process requiring the synthesis of PII, credit telemetry (FICO/SSN), and risk-weighting based on loan-to-value ratios. The challenge with standard AI is that regulated lending requires Equal Opportunity Compliance. We cannot rely on a purely probabilistic approach; we need a hybrid architecture that offers both agentic reasoning (to handle unstructured data) and deterministic nodes (to enforce rigid regulatory guardrails). This is precisely what the new Gemini Agent Designer provides.  | ![][image1] |

| :---- | :---- |

| We begin within the native workflow. As a Loan Officer, I’m leveraging Gemini Enterprise to surface high priority data- in this case pending loan applications.  \[Click new chat in assistant\]  Prompt: Do I have any new loan applications in my Inbox?    | ![][image2] |

| Prompt: @LoanSupervisor Can you process L\_0001? The team shared that workflow agents cannot be called from the chatbox, so this would have to be Vipin's loan supervisor or another no code agent that's not a workflow agent | ![][image3] (come back to thai screen after the output is generated |

| Let me show you what is happening in the backend. The backend is performing complex multi-agent orchestration. The 'Loan Supervisor' isn't a single prompt; it's an orchestrator calling four specialized sub-agents: \[Click on ‘Agent Engine’ in the console to show the runs\]  Document Validator: to check for presence of W-2’s, pay stubs etc based on a predefined checklist Credit Checker: to Look for a FICO score for loans Risk Assessor: To calculate a risk score for the applicant based on earlier applications, credit history etc.  Compliance Checker: To Validate the loan against company policies (e.g., self-employed applicants needing 2 years of tax returns) | ![][image4] |

| You will notice, this view also gives you detailed information on your agents runtime metrics, sessions and helps with tracing. All this is possible since each agent has a cryptographically secure identity which is used to set access and security policies for that agent. This avoids the risks of stale, over-permissioned service accounts. \[Public Preview at Next\]. All agents within an organization are also discoverable via the Registry and utilize the Agent Gateway as a centralized policy enforcement point. \[Preview at Next\] When registered in GE, we will also provide additional governance with respect to user level metrics to inform you of who in the company used which agent, when, popular agents etc. It also allows for integration with Model Armor and Sensitive data protection to prevent prompt injections, malicious intent, etc. | ![][image5] |

| (Returning to Gemini Enterprise)  We just demonstrated the processing of a single application. This architecture supports high-throughput asynchronous processing.  Now imagine this agent as a part of a bigger workflow that  can be triggered autonomously via API hooks, time-based schedulers, or system-of-record updates in platforms like Encompass (loan processing application). \[Click Agents on the left tab and click the ‘Loan Execution Agent’ in My Agents\]  You can now do this easily in our new Agent Designer workflow that empowers business users to automate their tasks and add conditions such ‘If then else’, human input, human review, add readymade skills and other lowcode and procode agents.   As we can see, the Loan Processing agent is now a part of a larger workflow that is automatically scheduled, streamlining manual operations.  Upon receiving a loan application, the information is processed and an action is taken based on the agent’s logic (which determines Risk Score based on loan amount, credit score, and income, etc). After the agent runs its logic, the workflow agent closes the loop by executing downstream actions such as automated approvals or rejections or storing conditional approvals for human review. |   ![][image6] \[the screenshot will change, this is of a different version\] |

| Let me show you the past runs for this agent. We can see that it has been processing applications.  \[Click on Runs tab\]  Here you can monitor errors, what's scheduled or completed  ~~\[Prompt: @Loanexecution agent Execute the decisions from the supervisor for the day.  This runs the low code agent which you will notice waits for human approval for certain steps, if desired.~~  | ![][image7] \[screenshot will change, this is of a different version\]  |

| \[Click on “Needs Review”\] We can also see the run that requires my review. I can click on any of them and take action.  Alternate: we can add a prompt asking the agent to show all applications that require my action. This can be displayed using A2UI followed by user-led actions to send email, create tickets etc.   | ![][image8] \[screenshot needs to change, action will not be scheduling cal\] |

| At the conclusion of a cycle, say, the officer needs to summarize a report of all the applications run by these agents.  \[Go back to chat, select Canvas and select Drive as the data source\]  Prompt: Generate a report with all the applications processed today. |  ![][image9]  |

|   Looking forward to Next ‘26, we are introducing additional features  lower the barrier to entry for the broader workforce: **Centralized Notifications:** An 'Agent Command Center' for monitoring scheduled tasks and exception handling across all my tasks and agents . **Modular Skills:** Reusable, governed components that ensure consistency across personal agents. **Agent Templates:** Pre-configured 'Starter Kits' to accelerate organizational adoption. **Project Spaces:** Shared collaborative environments that provide persistent context for teams working on high-value initiatives. Finally, we are also introducing **Long-Running Agents**, which move beyond session-based interactions to persistent, background execution of complex business processes." Close:  In summary, with Gemini Enterprise, we are offering a robust, governed and highly extensible platform to build the next generation of autonomous enterprise workflows.   |   ![][image10] ![][image11]  ![][image12]  ![][image13]    |
